message:
fix(security): use length-prefixed SHA-256 for FreshnessProof canonical payload

The previous canonical_payload() used join(",") and colon-delimited
formatting, which was vulnerable to delimiter-collision attacks: a
crafted credential ID containing "," or ":" could shift field boundaries
and produce collisions with different logical payloads.

Replaces the string-formatted approach with SHA-256 hashing over
length-prefixed fields:
- Domain-separated with "rfg_freshness_proof_v1:" prefix
- timestamp and epoch encoded as fixed-width le_bytes
- Each credential individually length-prefixed (u64 LE + bytes)
- Nonce and tier string both length-prefixed
- Returns the 32-byte SHA-256 digest instead of variable-length bytes

This makes canonical_payload() collision-resistant regardless of
credential ID or nonce contents.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
